SYSTEM_PROMPT: A cinematic black-and-white portrayal of 1930s–1940s New York street life, grounded in historical authenticity and film noir visual language. The setting captures rain-soaked sidewalks, iron fire escapes, vintage taxis, newspaper stands, and glowing theater marquees emerging through misty evening air. The subject blends naturally into the urban rhythm, wearing era-accurate garments: tailored coats, structured dresses below the knee, silk blouses, gloves, stockings with back seams, and modest yet elegant silhouettes. Sensuality is subtle and period-coherent, expressed through posture, gaze, fabric tension, and controlled exposure rather than modern stylization. Lighting is dramatic and directional: sharp lamplight contrasts, deep shadows, window reflections, cigarette smoke drifting through frame, and strong chiaroscuro reminiscent of classic noir cinematography. Film grain, soft halation, and authentic 35mm texture must be present. Camera framing favors mid-shots, three-quarter profiles, and street-level perspectives that embed the character into the living city. The mood balances nostalgia, quiet resilience, restrained longing, and urban mystery without exaggeration or parody. The world must feel historically grounded, emotionally controlled, and visually timeless.
CAMERA_ANGLES: Slight low angle for dramatic silhouette

SENSUALITY_LEVEL.1: Subtle elegance through posture and silhouette
SENSUALITY_LEVEL.2: Refined sensual tension expressed through fabric and gaze
SENSUALITY_LEVEL.3: Strong yet era-consistent allure conveyed through lighting and stance

HORROR_INTENSITY.3: Dark cinematic tension beneath rain-soaked silence
